title: 10 best herbal teas for gum infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth. The tea's essential oils, particularly terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to reduce plaque and gingivitis, while also reducing pain and swelling associated with gum infections. Additionally, tea tree teas can help to strengthen gum tissue and promote healthy oral hygiene, making it a natural and effective remedy for addressing gum infection and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea tree oil, which is often used to make tea tree teas, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, including those that cause gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in tea tree tea may help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, making it a natural remedy for reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants found in tea tree tea can help protect the mouth from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant are used to make medicinal teas for gum infection due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth.
Barks: The barks of the tea tree plant are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to dry up infected areas and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and steep them in one cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to infuse for five minutes then strain it into a clean cup.
Discard the solids and add two drops of peppermint essential oil for enhanced flavor and oral care benefits.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before using it as a mouthwash or gargling liquid.
Use the tea within one hour after preparation and store any leftover in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with gum infection because it contains potent antibacterial compounds that effectively combat bacteria causing periodontitis. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for faster healing of damaged tissues. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower tea help protect gums from further damage by neutralizing free radicals. By addressing these underlying causes, Kansas coneflower teas can be a valuable natural remedy for promoting healthy gums and preventing gum infection recurrences.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, easing discomfort and pain associated with gum infections.

Antibacterial properties: Kansas coneflower tea has been shown to possess antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial growth and inhibit the spread of infection, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further complications.

Wound-healing compounds: The tea contains wound-healing compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can accelerate the healing process by improving blood circulation, enhancing tissue repair, and reducing oxidative stress, all of which are essential for combating gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the echinacea mixture with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the herb particles.
Drink the tea three times a day, after meals and before bedtime, for at least two weeks to alleviate gum infection symptoms.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound eugenol in cloves has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and swelling in the gums. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help eliminate biofilms that can harbor harmful bacteria, promoting a healthy oral environment and aiding in the healing process. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe gum pain and reduce infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Cloves contain eugenol, which has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria that cause gum infections and reduce inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Eugenol in cloves also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Analgesic property: The essential oil of cloves contains analgesic compounds that can help alleviate toothache and other gum-related pains, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, clove roots help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with gum infections.
Rhyzomes: With their unique bioactive compounds, clove rhyzomes have antiseptic properties that aid in preventing the spread of infection and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves and flowers or purchase dried forms in suitable quantities for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried whole cloves or one tablespoon of loose-leaf clove mixture into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the clove ingredients and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes, allowing flavors to mingle.
Strain the tea carefully through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding any solids left behind.
Enjoy the warm, aromatic tea immediately, sipping slowly as needed throughout the day or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with gum infection because it is rich in antibacterial properties that effectively combat against the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning for optimal potency and antimicrobial properties.
Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of dried or fresh calendula flowers thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities.
Steep the calendula flowers in a teapot or infuser with 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 30 minutes before gargling and swishing it in your mouth for 1-2 minutes to help combat gum infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial properties, which target the bacteria that cause inflammation and swelling in the gums. remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a main component of wintergreen oil, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the gums, making it effective in soothing gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: Wintergreen tea contains compounds like methyl salicylate, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in wintergreen tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum infections, allowing the gums to heal faster.

Barks: Wintergreen barks contain salicylic acid, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh wintergreen leaves or 1 ounce dried wintergreen leaves.
Combine the harvested wintergreen leaves with 8 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding solids.
Drink 1 cup of the cooled tea three times daily for 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for the infection. remedy_recipe: Rinse dried danshen roots with cold running water to remove any impurities.
Measure out one teaspoon of the rinsed roots and combine it with eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a teapot or infuser to allow the herbs to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which combat bacteria that cause inflammation and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh ginger root and peel it using a vegetable peeler.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal teas is to steep dried sage leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and drink as a warm tea three times a day. Another option is to mix equal parts of chamomile and calendula flowers, steeping them in hot water for 10-15 minutes. This tea helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal teas are periodontal diseases, toothaches, and mouth sores. Teas made from herbs like sage, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the gums. Ginger tea is also used to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with toothaches and mouth sores, while peppermint tea helps freshen breath and reduce bad odors.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are a natural remedy that has been used to treat and prevent gum disease. These teas are made from herbs that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing in the gums. Examples of herbal teas that help with gum infection include peppermint, eucalyptus, and chamomile, which have been shown to improve oral health and reduce pain and discomfort. This can improve lives by reducing the risk of tooth loss and promoting overall well-being.
